title: peristylus turneri
intro: Peristylus turneri, also known as the Turners' orchid, is a species of orchid native to India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Peristylus turneri include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Peristylus turneri can be made by drying the plant's leaves and roots, and then using them to make teas, infusions, and decoctions.
